title: Ensure that the maximumRetainedFiles argument is set to 10 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the maximumRetainedFiles argument is set to 10 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Retain 10 or an appropriate number of old log files.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. For example, if you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would have approximately 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: OpenShift audit works at the API server level, logging all requests coming to the server. Configure via maximumRetainedFiles . Run the following command: oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -ojson | \ jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | \ jq -r .auditConfig.maximumRetainedFiles oc get configmap config -n openshift-apiserver -ojson | \ jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | \ jq -r .auditConfig.maximumRetainedFiles Verify that maximumRetainedFiles is set to 10 or as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Set the maximumRetainedFiles parameter to 10 or as an appropriate number of files. maximumRetainedFiles: 10
input_row.default_value: 114 | P a g e By default, auditing is enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit control specifies how to check a configuration property using OpenShift CLI commands, indicating that it can be verified programmatically.
- The commands provided (`oc get configmap ...`) utilize the OpenShift API to retrieve the configuration settings, showing that this property can be checked via IBM Cloud CLI commands.
- No SSH/RDP access is required, and the check is not about application code or visual inspection but rather about a configuration that can be queried through available APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_cluster_audit_log_maximum_retained_files

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Use IBM Cloud CLI with the `oc` command to solicit the current log file retention configuration.
- Execute the following OpenShift commands:
  1. `oc get configmap config -n openshift-kube-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | jq -r .auditConfig.maximumRetainedFiles`
  2. `oc get configmap config -n openshift-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.data["config.yaml"]' | jq -r .auditConfig.maximumRetainedFiles`
- Validate that the `maximumRetainedFiles` is set to `10` or an appropriate value as required.
- Pass criteria: If both commands yield a result of `10`, the configuration is compliant. If not, the configuration fails compliance.

The implementation of this automated check will efficiently verify the retention settings for OpenShift audit logs, ensuring compliance with the specified control.
